prompt:
A full-page illuminated manuscript miniature in the style of a 13th-14th century European book of hours or psalter, depicting a Decision Tree as a Tree of Knowledge.

The composition shows a large ornamental tree growing upward from a decorated root node at the bottom center of the page. The tree's trunk and branches are rendered as elaborate inhabited vine scrolls — intertwined acanthus leaves and decorative tendrils in malachite green and gold.

At each branching point of the tree, a circular gold-leaf roundel contains a miniature scene representing a decision node — a seated scholar figure examining or pointing at two diverging paths. The binary branching is clearly visible: each node splits into exactly two branches, left and right.

The leaf nodes at the top and outer edges of the tree are smaller roundels, each containing a distinct outcome symbol — a crown, a book, a key, a chalice, a sword, a flower — each rendered in vivid egg tempera colors against burnished gold backgrounds.

The entire composition is framed by a rectangular decorated border of acanthus leaves, ivy vines, and small gold rosettes at the four corners. The background within the frame is filled with burnished gold leaf and subtle diaper patterns. The space outside the border shows warm aged parchment/vellum texture.

Color palette strictly limited to: burnished gold leaf, ultramarine blue (lapis lazuli), vermillion red, malachite green, lead white, and lamp black outlines. Flat perspective with no vanishing point. Hierarchical scaling — the root node roundel is largest, leaf node roundels are smallest. Horror vacui — every surface filled with decoration. Visible fine ink outlines beneath painted areas. Egg tempera brushwork texture on vellum.

No text, no modern elements, no labels, no mathematical notation, no digital artifacts. Authentic medieval manuscript illumination style. Aspect ratio 1:1. Ultra-high detail. 2048x2048 resolution.
tradition_approach: Decision tree as inhabited vine-scroll Tree of Knowledge, nodes as gold-leaf roundels with miniature figures, leaf outcomes as heraldic symbols, acanthus borders, ultramarine-vermillion palette on vellum, flat hierarchical composition with horror vacui.
